description_long: This is a delicate purple flower with six prominent, slightly overlapping petals that form a cup-like shape. The petals are arranged radially, creating a symmetrical and elegant bloom. At the center, slender yellow-orange stamens are visible, adding a contrasting accent to the deep purple hue of the petals. The flower is supported by a slender green stem, from which a few narrow, grass-like leaves emerge and curve gently outward. The surface of the petals appears smooth with subtle veining, while the stem and leaves have a matte, slightly textured finish. This flower would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped sidewalks, adding a touch of natural beauty to the environment.
description: A vibrant purple flower with six radially arranged petals, yellow-orange stamens, and slender green leaves on a single stem. The bloom is cup-shaped and visually striking.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cup-shaped bloom with all six petals visible and two leaves extending outward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the central stamens and the curvature of the petals, with one leaf more prominently angled forward.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the petals and the stem, with leaves splaying outward and less of the flower's center visible.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the overlapping petals and the arrangement of the leaves, with a partial glimpse of the stamens.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with slight overlapping at the base, forming a symmetrical cup-like structure typical of crocus or similar flowers.
